title: Ophrys × buttleri
intro: Ophrys × buttleri, also known as the Monkey Orchid or Ophrys butleriana, is a hybrid orchid species that belongs to the Ophrys genus, characterized by its unique, exotic flowers resembling female insects. The health benefits of Ophrys × buttleri include its potential to aid in respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and bronchodilatory properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Ophrys × buttleri include teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used in traditional medicine to treat respiratory disorders and as a natural expectorant.
taxonomy: Ophrys × buttleri is a hybrid orchid that belongs to the family Orchidaceae. This unique plant is a cross between Ophrys apifera and Ophrys sphegodes, resulting in a distinctive appearance. The classification of Ophrys × buttleri is as a nothospecies within the Ophrys genus. It is often found in the Iberian Peninsula, where its parent species also inhabit. As a hybrid, Ophrys × buttleri is not considered to be a separate species in the classical sense.
